summary: Added a dependency-free mockable madobe-hyprland crate with an injectable hyprctl command executor, typed errors that preserve command context, fixture-backed monitor/workspace parsing, and a small socket2 event parsing layer.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Command executor is injectable for tests.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: CommandExecutor trait and HyprlandClient<E>; crates/hyprland/tests/fixtures.rs uses FixtureExecutor.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Fixture-backed tests cover monitor and workspace parsing.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: crates/hyprland/tests/fixtures.rs reads fixtures/hyprland/monitors.json, workspaces.json, and activeworkspace.json.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Errors are typed and preserve command context.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: HyprlandErrorKind plus CommandContext; parse_errors_preserve_command_context test asserts hyprctl arguments are retained.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: No product code shells out directly except through the command layer.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: rg -n "std::process::Command|Command::new" crates apps returns only crates/hyprland/src/command.rs.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: No new third-party dependency policy exception is required.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: The serde/serde_json dependency graph was removed; nix develop -c just security passes and cargo-vet reports no third-party dependencies.

residualRisks: Live Hyprland socket and hardware lifecycle behavior are not exercised in this node.
The event layer parses socket2 lines but does not yet own a live Unix socket connection.
The internal JSON parser is intentionally scoped to the current Hyprland hyprctl fixture shapes.

realWorldValidation.evidence: Command-layer node only; live Hyprland socket and hardware lifecycle behavior are deferred.
